---
title: Modelling Bike Activity within The Beach BIA
date: today
date-format: long
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---


```{r}
#| label: setup-and-prep
#| include: false

# Load Libraries
library(opendatatoronto)
library(dplyr)
library(conflicted)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(here)
library(janitor)
library(zoo)
library(suncalc)
library(segmented)
library(MASS)
library(broom)
library(kableExtra)
library(arrow)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)
```


```{r}
# Read Files
clean_path <- here("01-data", "clean")

beaches_bike_data    <- readRDS(file.path(clean_path, "beaches_bike_trips.rds"))
beaches_water_status <- read_parquet(file.path(clean_path, "beach_water_quality.parquet"))
climate_daily        <- read_parquet(file.path(clean_path, "toronto_weather_daily.parquet"))
sun_data             <- read_parquet(file.path(clean_path, "toronto_solar_data.parquet"))


# Aggregate Bike Trips (2025)
daily_trips_2025 <- beaches_bike_data %>%
  filter(year == 2025) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(dt_start_time)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    total_trips = n(),
    avg_duration_sec = mean(trip_duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_casual = sum(user_type == "Casual Member") / n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


holidays_2025 <- as.Date(c(
  "2025-01-01", "2025-02-17", "2025-04-18", "2025-05-19", 
  "2025-07-01", "2025-08-04", "2025-09-01", "2025-10-13",
  "2025-12-25", "2025-12-26"
))

# Merge model data

model_data_2025 <- daily_trips_2025 %>%
  left_join(climate_daily, by = "date") %>%
  left_join(sun_data %>% dplyr::select(date, daylight_hours, usable_light_hours), by = "date") %>%
  left_join(beaches_water_status %>% rename(date = Date) %>% mutate(date = as.Date(date)), by = "date") %>%
  mutate(
    is_weekend = ifelse(wday(date) %in% c(1, 7), 1, 0),
    is_holiday = ifelse(date %in% holidays_2025, 1, 0),
    beach_closed = case_when(
      is.na(closed_kew) & is.na(closed_woodbine) ~ 0,
      closed_kew & closed_woodbine ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    max_ecoli = pmax(ecoli_kew, ecoli_woodbine, na.rm = TRUE),
    log_ecoli = log(max_ecoli + 1),
    time_index = as.numeric(date - min(date))
  )
```


```{r}
#| fig.width: 8
#| fig.height: 5

# Fit the models
fit_lin_temp <- lm(total_trips ~ daily_mean_temp, data = model_data_2025)
fit_seg_temp <- segmented(fit_lin_temp, seg.Z = ~daily_mean_temp, npsi = 1)
hinge_temp <- fit_seg_temp$psi[2]

model_data_2025$predicted_trips <- predict(fit_seg_temp)

ggplot(model_data_2025, aes(x = daily_mean_temp, y = total_trips)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "gray50") +
  geom_line(aes(y = predicted_trips), color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = hinge_temp, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  annotate("text", x = hinge_temp + 2, y = max(model_data_2025$total_trips) * 0.9, 
           label = paste0("Activation: ", round(hinge_temp, 1), "°C"), fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "The Temperature 'Hinge' for Beach Ridership",
    subtitle = "Identifying the threshold where ridership shifts from baseline to rapid growth",
    x = "Daily Mean Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Total Daily Trips"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```



```{r}
fit_annual <- glm.nb(
  total_trips ~ daily_mean_temp + daily_total_precip + daylight_hours + is_weekend + daily_mean_temp*is_weekend, 
  data = model_data_2025
)

# Format the output into a clean presentation table
tidy(fit_annual, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(Term = term, `Rate Multiplier (Exp)` = estimate, `P-Value` = p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    Term = case_when(
      Term == "(Intercept)" ~ "Baseline (Intercept)",
      Term == "daily_mean_temp" ~ "Mean Temperature (°C)",
      Term == "daily_total_precip" ~ "Total Precipitation (mm)",
      Term == "daylight_hours" ~ "Daylight Hours",
      Term == "is_weekend" ~ "Weekend Indicator",
      Term == "daily_mean_temp:is_weekend" ~ "Temp x Weekend Interaction",
      TRUE ~ Term
    ),
    `P-Value` = cell_spec(round(`P-Value`, 4), bold = `P-Value` < 0.05, color = ifelse(`P-Value` < 0.05, "green", "red"))
  ) %>%
  kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE, caption = "Annual Baseline Predictors of Bike Demand") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = F)
```



```{r}
library(mgcv)
# s() creates a spline. bs="cc" is a cyclic cubic spline (makes Dec 31 connect smoothly to Jan 1)
fit_gam <- gam(total_trips ~ s(daily_mean_temp) + 
                             s(time_index, bs="cc") + # captures seasonal wave
                             is_weekend + 
                             daily_total_precip, 
               family = nb(), 
               data = model_data_2025)
```



```{r}
fit_gamm <- gamm(total_trips ~ s(daily_mean_temp) + is_weekend, 
                 family = nb(), 
                 correlation = corAR1(form = ~ time_index), 
                 data = model_data_2025)
```



```{r}
nb_resid <- residuals(fit_annual, type = "pearson")

# 2. Plot the Autocorrelation Function
# We set lag.max to 30 to see up to a month of lagged effects
acf(nb_resid, lag.max = 30, 
    main = "ACF of NB Model Residuals (fit_annual)", 
    lwd = 2)
```




```{r}
library(mgcv)

# Assuming you have combined 2022-2026 into 'model_data_all'
# and created a 'year' factor and a continuous 'time_index' 

fit_gamm <- gamm(
  total_trips ~ s(daily_mean_temp) +      # Spline for non-linear temp effects
                s(daylight_hours) + 
                daily_total_precip + 
                is_weekend,         # Step-functions for YoY network growth
  family = nb(),                          # Negative Binomial distribution
  correlation = corAR1(form = ~ time_index), # Handles the Lag 1 spike
  data = model_data_2025
)

# To view the summary of the fixed effects:
summary(fit_gamm$gam)

# To check if the AR1 term successfully flattened the residuals:
acf(residuals(fit_gamm$lme, type = "normalized"), main = "ACF of GAMM Residuals")

fit_gamm_2025 <- fit_gamm
```


```{r}
# pages = 1 puts all the splines on a single plot
# shade = TRUE adds the standard error confidence bands
# all.terms = TRUE includes the linear parametric terms too
plot(fit_gamm_2025$gam, pages = 1, shade = TRUE, shade.col = "lightblue", all.terms = TRUE)
# install.packages("gratia")
library(gratia)

# Automatically plots all smooths with partial residuals and confidence intervals
draw(fit_gamm_2025$gam, residuals = TRUE)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

fit_gamm_2025 <- fit_gamm
# 1. Setup synthetic data for an "Average" 2025 Day
avg_temp <- mean(model_data_2025$daily_mean_temp, na.rm = TRUE)
avg_daylight <- mean(model_data_2025$daylight_hours, na.rm = TRUE)
median_time <- median(model_data_2025$time_index, na.rm = TRUE) # Needed for model structure

sim_weekend <- data.frame(
  is_weekend = c(0, 1), # Compare Weekday (0) vs Weekend (1)
  daily_mean_temp = avg_temp,
  daylight_hours = avg_daylight,
  daily_total_precip = 0, # Dry day
  time_index = median_time
)

# 2. Predict Trips (type = "response" gives actual expected counts)
sim_weekend$predicted_trips <- predict(fit_gamm_2025$gam, newdata = sim_weekend, type = "response")

# 3. Format data for plotting
sim_weekend <- sim_weekend %>%
  mutate(DayType = ifelse(is_weekend == 1, "Weekend", "Weekday"))

# 4. Generate the Plot
ggplot(sim_weekend, aes(x = DayType, y = predicted_trips, fill = DayType)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(predicted_trips)), vjust = -0.5, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#95a5a6", "#e67e22")) + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) + # Gives text breathing room
  labs(
    title = "The Weekend Surge in The Beach",
    subtitle = paste0("Expected bike trips on an average (", round(avg_temp, 1), "°C) dry day"),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Expected Bike Trips"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```



```{r}
# 1. Setup synthetic data: Vary daylight over the 2025 range
sim_daylight <- data.frame(
  daylight_hours = seq(min(model_data_2025$daylight_hours, na.rm = TRUE), 
                       max(model_data_2025$daylight_hours, na.rm = TRUE), 
                       length.out = 100),
  daily_mean_temp = 15,    # Holding temp at a pleasant 15C
  daily_total_precip = 0,  # Dry
  is_weekend = 1,          # Weekend
  time_index = median_time
)

# 2. Predict Trips
sim_daylight$predicted_trips <- predict(fit_gamm_2025$gam, newdata = sim_daylight, type = "response")

# 3. Generate the Plot
ggplot(sim_daylight, aes(x = daylight_hours, y = predicted_trips)) +
  # Highlight the low-light penalty zone
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 11, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.1, fill = "black") +
  annotate("text", x = 10.5, y = max(sim_daylight$predicted_trips) * 0.5, 
           label = "Short Day\nPenalty Zone", fontface = "bold", color = "gray40", angle = 90) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 1.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Daylight Acts as a Ceiling on Traffic",
    subtitle = "Predicted trips on a 15°C dry weekend",
    x = "Hours of Usable Daylight",
    y = "Expected Bike Trips"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(9, 16, by = 1))
```



```{r}
summer_clean <- model_data_2025 %>%
  filter(!is.na(ecoli_woodbine) & !is.na(ecoli_kew))

fit_summer_final <- glm.nb(
  total_trips ~ daily_mean_temp + daily_total_precip + is_weekend + beach_closed, 
  data = summer_clean
)

tidy(fit_summer_final, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  select(Term = term, `Rate Multiplier` = estimate, `P-Value` = p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    Term = case_when(
      Term == "(Intercept)" ~ "Baseline",
      Term == "daily_mean_temp" ~ "Mean Temperature (°C)",
      Term == "daily_total_precip" ~ "Total Precipitation (mm)",
      Term == "is_weekend" ~ "Weekend Indicator",
      Term == "beach_closed" ~ "Beach Closed (Indicator)",
      Term == "log_ecoli" ~ "Log(Max E. Coli)",
      TRUE ~ Term
    ),
    `P-Value` = cell_spec(round(`P-Value`, 4), bold = `P-Value` < 0.05, color = ifelse(`P-Value` < 0.05, "green", "red"))
  ) %>%
  kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE, caption = "Impact of Water Quality on Summer Cycling Demand") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = F)

```

```{r}
library(MASS) # Essential for glm.nb

# Running the Negative Binomial Regression
fit_annual <- glm.nb(total_trips ~ daily_mean_temp + daily_total_precip + daylight_hours + 
                     is_weekend + daily_mean_temp * is_weekend, 
                     data = model_data_2025) # Replace with your actual DF name

summary(fit_annual)

# Convert logs to readable percentages
exp(coef(fit_annual))
```








```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Create a "synthetic" average day for the BIA
# We hold everything constant except temperature to isolate the effect
sim_data_temp <- data.frame(
  daily_mean_temp = seq(min(model_data_2025$daily_mean_temp, na.rm = TRUE), 
                        max(model_data_2025$daily_mean_temp, na.rm = TRUE), 
                        length.out = 100),
  daylight_hours = mean(model_data_2025$daylight_hours, na.rm = TRUE),
  daily_total_precip = 0, # Assuming a dry day
  is_weekend = 1    # Looking at peak weekend traffic          # Setting to your most recent full year
)

# 2. Predict actual trips using your GAMM
# type = "response" converts the log-link back into actual trip counts!
sim_data_temp$predicted_trips <- predict(fit_gamm$gam, newdata = sim_data_temp, type = "response")

# 3. Build the Business-Friendly Plot
ggplot(sim_data_temp, aes(x = daily_mean_temp, y = predicted_trips)) +
  geom_line(color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1.5) +
  # Adding a shaded region for the "Prime Activation Zone" (e.g., 15C to 25C)
  annotate("rect", xmin = 15, xmax = 25, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.1, fill = "orange") +
  annotate("text", x = 20, y = max(sim_data_temp$predicted_trips) * 0.9, 
           label = "Prime Retail Zone", fontface = "bold", color = "#d35400") +
  labs(
    title = "The Temperature Tipping Point for BIA Traffic",
    subtitle = "Predicted daily bike trips on a dry weekend (2025 Baseline)",
    x = "Daily Average Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Expected Bike Trips"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-10, 30, by = 5))
```
